meta.setting: Frosted hay meadow at the edge of cougar country
meta.rdr_clone_target: PS3-era RDR-clone western art, grounded in hunting restraint, food pressure, and wildlife sign

research_grounding.title: California Department of Fish and Wildlife Deer
research_grounding.use: Deer presence, track sign, and range context.

research_grounding.title: California Department of Fish and Wildlife Elk
research_grounding.use: Elk herd and landscape pressure context.

research_grounding.title: California Department of Fish and Wildlife Mountain Lion

image_prompt.subject: black-tailed deer and distant elk moving through a hay meadow while riders stay low near a fence, with cougar country rising behind
image_prompt.scene: early morning mist; frost on grass, old hay stacks, oak edge, rifle kept down, birds lifting, and a faint drag mark disappearing into brush at one corner
image_prompt.composition: square meadow scene with animals spread naturally and humans small; hay and tracks foreground, herd middle distance, predator brush line behind
image_prompt.camera: telephoto-like game camera from behind grass, readable but not photorealistic, with the riders partially hidden
material_cues: animal hides in muted morning light
oak leaves at the brush edge
image_prompt.hand_cut_note: Make restraint visible: the most interesting choice is not always the shot.

style_modifiers.medium: PS3-era open-world western game art, early-HD in-engine wildlife and hunting scene
render_traits: slightly simplified animal and rider silhouettes
modest texture resolution on fur, grass, frost, wood, and wool
hard ambient occlusion under animals, hay stacks, fence rails, and brush
soft cold morning light with mist
believable 1899 hunting posture and trail gear
naturalistic herd behavior rather than trophy composition
style_modifiers.palette: frost white, hay gold, deer brown, elk dark tan, oak green, wool gray, cold mist blue, and brush shadow.

text_rendering.instructions: No readable signs, labels, tracking overlays, or text.

negative_prompt: copy of commercial game characters, missions, towns, or logos

self_check: Reads as PS3-era western game art at square page-card scale.
The deer, elk, riders, hay meadow, fence, and brush line are fully readable.
Every visible prop belongs plausibly to 1899 hunting and meadow life.
The image communicates food pressure and restraint, not trophy hunting.
No copied game characters, readable modern text, or inherited non-western symbols.
